rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH RILEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, November 5, 2010 in Department 220 at 10:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence, strict liability and punitive damages and granted as to false representation. Plaintiff¿s declaration and deposition and the depositions of Mr. Germain create triable issuee whether plaintiff worked with internal components of new Allen-Bradley products and whether defendant acted with malice or oppression. Plaintiff¿s discovery responses were factually devoid and he failed to present evidence creating a triable issue that any actionable representation was made by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH ROUSH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Crane Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, November 5, 2010 in Department 220 at 10:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether his removal of packing from one or two valves attributable to defendant is a substantial factor in causing his asbestos-related illness. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CASEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Turner Construction Company'S Motion For Order Of Protection Against Deposition Notice Of Turner Construction Company'S Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Record And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, November 5, 2010 in Department 220 at 10:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Turner Construction Company's Motion For Order Of Protection Against Deposition of Turner Construction's Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Record And Request For Production Of Documents. Defendant Turner Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Casey was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance is denied. In light of the tentative ruling on the motion for summary judgment, the two discovery motions to be heard concurrently with the summary judgment motion will go off calendar. The court has already received a notice that plaintiffs will contest this tentative ruling, so no further notice is required. = (220/HEK)
